hypothesis: When one model endpoint has a fast body and an asymmetric timeout tail, completed-only p95 latency will rank it ahead of a steadier endpoint even when a deadline-aware p95 and completion rate rank it behind; the reversal should disappear when the tail is ablated.
claimBoundary: The study demonstrates estimator behavior under the declared synthetic latency distributions and deadlines. It does not estimate any named provider's latency, prove that deadline-aware p95 alone is sufficient, or select a universal timeout.

baselines: steady endpoint with lognormal latency and no injected long-tail component
latent uncensored p95 computed from every simulated completion time
controls: fast endpoint with the six-percent long-tail component removed
paired random draws shared by endpoint conditions within every repeat
